fix: real upload progress via XHR for compress and convert

Replace fake progress interval (2% every 1.5s) with XHR-based real progress:
- 5%→65%: actual upload progress (bytes sent / total)
- 66%→90%: slow tick during FFmpeg processing phase
- 95%: when response arrives
- Status message changes: "Uploading..." → "Compressing/Converting with FFmpeg..."

Users can now see accurate progress during large file uploads instead
of the bar freezing at 90% for minutes.

commit.message:
Add dual extruder control, temperature store, and Klipper command interception

- Intercept Klipper commands (ACTIVATE_EXTRUDER, SET_HEATER_TEMPERATURE,
  TURN_OFF_HEATERS, SET_GCODE_OFFSET) and route through SACP
- Intercept M104/M109/M140/M190 to use SACP SetToolTemperature directly
- Track active extruder in state, reflect in toolhead.extruder object
- Implement ring buffer temperature store (1200 readings per sensor)
  so Mainsail graphs persist across tab switches
- Fix GCode post-processor: per-tool absolute E tracking, per-extruder
  retraction value parsing for dual-extruder jobs
- SET_GCODE_OFFSET silently accepted (J1S firmware lacks baby-stepping)

commit.message:
Reproduce Bug 2: client-driven probe finds Wasm memory threshold

The memory crash IS real but depends on Worker package footprint.
A single-endpoint /probe accumulated memory across steps and gave
misleading results. Replaced with /probe/seed/{mb} + /probe/roundtrip/{mb}
so each step runs in a fresh isolate.

Results from deployed testing (2026-03-05):
- Issue 4 Worker (336 modules / 8.5MB): crashes at 60MB
- Tasche staging (457 modules / 9.5MB): crashes at 30-42MB (flaky)

The threshold is not fixed — it varies with GC state and other
per-request allocations. Heavier Workers crash at lower sizes because
the baseline memory footprint leaves less Wasm headroom.

README updated with cross-validation data from Tasche staging.
Test uses client-driven loop instead of single /probe endpoint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: V2.4 extraction — detached subprocess instead of daemon thread

Root cause: output_json() calls sys.exit(0) before background thread
starts, AND hook timeout is only 3s (extraction needs ~30s).

Solution: spawn extract-worker.py as independent subprocess via
Popen(start_new_session=True) BEFORE output_json. Subprocess
survives both sys.exit and hook timeout.

- POST /auth/register, /auth/login, /auth/refresh 엔드포인트 추가
- passlib[bcrypt] 비밀번호 해싱, python-jose JWT 토큰 서명
- refresh token rotation 및 DB 저장/무효화
- 모든 Job 엔드포인트에 Depends(get_current_user) 적용

- 인증 없이 보호 엔드포인트 호출 시 401 반환
- 92개 테스트 통과 (신규 auth 19개 포함)

closes #98

* fix: CI 린트 오류 수정 (ruff format, eslint)

- ruff format 적용 (auth.py, db.py, main.py, test_api.py, test_rate_limit.py)
- App.tsx: 미사용 startPolling 제거
- useJobWebSocket.ts: ref 업데이트를 useEffect로 이동

* fix: bcrypt<4.1 핀으로 passlib 호환성 문제 해결

passlib의 detect_wrap_bug()가 256바이트 테스트 비밀번호를 사용하는데
bcrypt 4.1+에서 72바이트 제한을 엄격히 적용하여 오류 발생.

commit.message:
Fix CI releasing by failing on duplicate version tags (#60)

The release job silently re-uploaded artifacts to the same release when the
version wasn't bumped, so no new releases were created since v0.1.1. Now the
job fails with a clear error if the tag already exists. Bumps version to 0.1.2.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com> via Dash <dash@syv.ai>

Release v1.1.0

Add opt-in lint integration (lint, lint_severity, lint_plan inputs),
security hardening (randomized GITHUB_OUTPUT delimiters, env-based tag
passing, token validation), and documentation updates. Pin bats-core
action to SHA; use version tags for GitHub-owned actions.

commit.message:
Add Qwen3.5-27B fine-tuning notebook and update docs

New notebook: Qwen3_5_27B_JordanPeterson_FineTuning.ipynb
- First Qwen3.5 model in the repo (hybrid DeltaNet + full attention, 64 layers)
- Loads via FastVisionModel with vision layers frozen for text-only LoRA
- Uses same V4 Q&A cache (3,936 pairs), r=32, 3 epochs
- batch_size=1, grad_accum=8 to fit within 24 GB VRAM
- OOM handler with fallback to Qwen3.5-9B

Library updates: unsloth 2026.2.1→2026.3.3, transformers 4.57.6→5.3.0

commit.message:
ML Lab Phase 3: Feature engineering overhaul + triple barrier labeling

Feature Engineering:
- Rewrite features.py with NumPy vectorization (10-50x speedup)
- Add time features: cyclical hour-of-day and day-of-week (sin/cos)
- Add regime features: ATR ratio, return autocorrelation, vol clustering
- Add momentum features: rate of change (5/10/20), price acceleration
- Add rolling Z-score normalization (preserves temporal structure)

Triple Barrier Labeling:
- New target type: SL/TP/time barriers using ATR-based distances
- Labels: 1.0 (TP hit), 0.0 (SL hit), 0.5 (time expired)
- Configurable sl_atr_mult, tp_atr_mult, max_holding_bars

Data Pipeline:
- Parse timestamps from CSV (MT5, ISO, standard formats)
- Thread timestamps through trainer, predict, and ml_filter
- Support multiclass metrics (weighted avg) for 3-class targets

Frontend:
- Add triple barrier target option with SL/TP/hold controls
- Add normalization dropdown (none/zscore) with window config
- Updated feature catalogue shows 16 feature groups

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(android): full Telegram-like voice UX — preview, lock mode, send/delete/listen

VoiceState machine: IDLE → RECORDING → LOCKED | PREVIEW → IDLE

RECORDING (holding mic):
- Overlay shows ← Отмена + ● timer + 🔒 hint
- Swipe LEFT > 80dp → cancel immediately
- Swipe UP > 80dp → LOCKED mode (recording continues hands-free)
- Release finger → PREVIEW mode

LOCKED (slid up, recording without holding):
- Bottom bar: [🔒 ● timer] [❌ Cancel] [▶ Send immediately]

PREVIEW (finger released normally):
- Bottom bar: [🗑 Delete] [▶/⏸ Play + progress + duration] [▶ Send]
- User can listen before sending, or delete

ChatViewModel: VoiceState enum, lockRecording, stopRecordingToPreview,
sendVoiceFromLocked, sendVoicePreview, deleteVoicePreview,
togglePreviewPlayback, uploadVoice helper

commit.message:
feat(scanner): structural signal system upgrade (Phases 1-5)

Phase 1: ADX + regime classification (TRENDING/RANGING/VOLATILE), ATR-based
exit levels (SL/TP1/TP2), time-based watchlist exit, DB fields for entryPrice
and exitReason. Discord and feed UI surface regime badge and exit plan.

Phase 2: Replace 4-pillar scoring with 3-pillar system (Derivatives/Structure/
Technicals, 0-300). Add position sizing (risk%, position%, dollarRisk10k).
MIN_SCORE lowered to 225. All downstream consumers updated.

Phase 3: Adaptive thresholds via 500-sample ring buffer. RSI bounds use
15th/85th percentile; volume spike uses 90th percentile of recent readings.

Phase 4: Hyperliquid on-chain integration (single metaAndAssetCtxs call per
scan). Funding divergence risk flag, OI confirmation bonus. Feature-flagged
via ENABLE_HYPERLIQUID env var.

Phase 5: Offline backtesting framework (apps/backtest). Walk-forward engine
with train/test windows, Monte Carlo threshold perturbation (N=100, ±15%),
CLI entry. Pure scoring functions extracted to scoring-functions.ts.

commit.message:
Restore fence regex to \w+ to prevent false positive unclosed-fence warnings

The \w* pattern matches closing fences as "opens" (bare ``` followed by
newline), causing false positive quality corrections on balanced code.
Using \w+ (require language tag) avoids this; bare opening fences without
tags are rare in practice and the trade-off favors fewer false positives.

refactor: replace Qt headers with STL/Boost equivalents (#8840)

Replace 9 Qt header includes across 8 files with standard C++/Boost
alternatives to reduce Qt coupling:

- QRegularExpression → std::regex (MascotGenericFile, InspectOutfile)
- QFile + QTextStream → std::ifstream (3 CHEMISTRY data providers)
- QCryptographicHash + QFile → self-contained SHA1 + std::ifstream (FileHandler)
- Remove dead includes: QDebug (File.cpp), QDir (MultiplexClustering.cpp)

commit.message:
feat: MW/MWh inputs, Baltic prices 45/40/22, scaled alloc/OPEX/aug, pct grants, S2 fleet list

Worker computeRevenue:
- Add mwParam/mwhParam so any system size can be modelled
- FCR/aFRR/mFRR proxy prices: 20/15/11 → 45/40/22 (market-calibrated)
- Alloc: fixed table → proportional (16% FCR, 34% aFRR, 50% mFRR of MW)
- OPEX: 1.95M fixed → mw × 39k (scales with system size)
- Augmentation capex: 3M fixed → mwh × 25k (scales with storage)
- CAPEX low: €98 → €120/kWh, relabelled
- Grants: amount-based → pct-based (none/partial 10%/full 30%)
- CPI MATURE floor: 0.35 → 0.40

Worker /revenue handler:
- Parse mw/mwh query params, pass to computeRevenue
- r2h/r4h compat calls use system defaults (mw=0 → sys.mw)

RevenueCard:
- Replace system state (2.4h/4h) with mw/mwh states (default 50/100)
- [2H][4H] preset pills + custom MW/MWh number inputs
- Grant pills: none/10%/30% (maps to none/partial/full)
- CAPEX pills show €120/€164/€262
- fkMw calls pass actual mw; subtitle shows mw/mwh + 20yr model

S2Card:
- Add FleetEntry interface; FleetCountry.entries
- Render Baltic BESS fleet list after trajectory bars (sorted by status)
- Colors: operational=teal, under_construction=amber, else=ghost

Fleet reseeded with demand=1190 MW
